title: Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Enhancement Act
short_title: Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Enhancement Act
plain_summary: This bill requires the Department of Agriculture to hire a college or university to study a program that fights cattle fever ticks. These ticks spread a dangerous disease to cattle called bovine babesiosis or cattle fever. The study must look at how well the program works to stop the disease, how it affects cattle farmers, what treatments are used, and how much money the government spends on it. The college must then write a report with their findings and suggestions for making the program better and give it to Congress within two years.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Livestock, Dairy, and Poultry.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Enhancement Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Review and Report
sections.text:
The Secretary of Agriculture must hire a college or university to study how well the Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program is working. This study must be started within one year after this law is passed.

The study must look at several things. It must check how well the program stops tick diseases from spreading in cattle. It must look at what benefits cattle farmers get from the program and what problems or extra work the program creates for them. The study must also review the treatment methods used in the program and check how much money the federal government and states spend on the program each year, including money for research projects.

Within one year after the Secretary of Agriculture hires the college or university, the Secretary must send a report to Congress. This report must include the results of the study and suggestions for making the program better, especially ways to make it easier for cattle farmers to follow the program rules.

A covered institution means either a land grant college or university, or a non land grant college of agriculture. A land grant college or university is a type of school defined in an existing federal law about agricultural research and education. A non land grant college of agriculture is also defined in that same law. The Program means the Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program that is run by the Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service of the Department of Agriculture working together with the Texas Animal Health Commission.
